description: Name of the metadata column/field as it appears in curated metadata files.
notes: Serves as the column header in metadata CSV files and is the primary key for schema lookup.

description: Expected R data class for values in this column.
notes: Used to coerce and validate values during import. 'double' and 'numeric' both allow decimal numbers; 'integer' restricts to whole numbers.

description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all samples in a dataset.
notes: Currently only sample_id is marked 'unique'. All other fields are 'non-unique'.

description: Whether this field must be populated (non-NA) for every sample.
notes: Fields marked 'required' must have a value for every row in the curated metadata.

description: Whether a single cell may contain multiple values separated by a delimiter.
notes: When TRUE, inspect the 'delimiter' or 'separator' column to determine which character separates multiple values.

description: Human-readable explanation of the field's purpose, content, and curation rules.
notes: May include ontology references (e.g., NCIT:XXXXXXX), curation instructions, and notes on lookup strategy or allowed value conditionals.

description: Defines valid values for the field. Interpretation depends on corpus.type: a regex pattern for regexp/integer/numeric fields, or pipe-separated enumerated terms for enum fields.
notes: NA when values are unrestricted (corpus.type = 'any') or when values are fetched dynamically from an ontology (corpus.type = 'dynamic_enum' only).

description: Pipe-separated ontology term IDs that correspond 1-to-1 with the terms listed in 'allowedvalues'.
notes: Provides ontology grounding for static enum values. Order must match the order of terms in 'allowedvalues'. NA for non-enum fields.

description: Semicolon-separated seed ontology term ID(s) used as root nodes for dynamic term lookup.
notes: At runtime, valid values are fetched by traversing the ontology graph from these seed IDs. NA for non-dynamic-enum fields.

description: Ontology traversal strategy applied to the dynamic.enum seed IDs when fetching valid terms.
notes: 'descendant' retrieves all levels below the seed; 'children' retrieves only direct child terms. NA for non-dynamic-enum fields.

description: Character sequence used to separate multiple paired/linked values within a cell.
notes: Used for fields whose values are positionally paired with values in a linked field (e.g., probing_pocket_depth and probing_pocket_depth_value share <;> so that multi-value entries remain aligned). Distinct from 'separator'.

description: Character used to separate multiple independent values within a cell.
notes: Used when a field contains several independent values (e.g., multiple diseases, multiple curators). Distinct from 'delimiter', which is reserved for positionally paired multi-value fields.

description: Classification that determines which validation rule is applied to the field's values.
allowed_values: regexp | integer | numeric | static_enum | dynamic_enum | dynamic_enum;static_enum | binary | custom_enum | character | any
notes: regexp: must match the regex in allowedvalues. integer/numeric: must be the stated numeric type. static_enum: must be one of the pipe-separated terms in allowedvalues (ontology-backed). dynamic_enum: must be a term retrieved from the ontology via dynamic.enum seeds. dynamic_enum;static_enum: combines both lookup and fixed static terms. binary: must be 'Yes' or 'No'. custom_enum: must be one of the pipe-separated terms in allowedvalues (curated, not ontology-backed). character: string with pattern validation via allowedvalues. any: no specific validation.

description: Sort position of this field within its display group for user interface presentation.
notes: Lower values appear first. Numbering is scoped within each display.group, not globally.

description: Logical group that organises fields for display and navigation purposes.
allowed_values: 00 Identifiers | 01 Sampling / Study | 02 Sequencing / QC | 03 Subject / Demographics | 04 Clinical / Intervention | 05 Other
notes: The numeric prefix controls the sort order of groups. Fields within the same group share a thematic relationship.
